recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO is the strongest 5D and 30D performer in the universe (+13.3%, +10.4%), approaching its 30D high near $359. Strong semiconductor momentum alongside NVDA. Deploying ~$5K of remaining cash into this position.
NVDA shows a clean uptrend above its 20-SMA with +1.0% today and consistent 30-day performance. Adding a 10% position to gain semiconductor exposure with strong chart structure.
GOOGL is up +1.6% today, the strongest performer among mega-cap tech, with a solid 30D trend of +1.58% and price near 30-day highs. Increasing from 12% to 15% to capture continued momentum.
WMT at +1.4% today near its 30-day high provides defensive consumer staples exposure. The chart shows a steady uptrend from 126.82 to 129.44 above rising SMA, offering portfolio ballast.
JPM is up +1.2% today and near its 30-day high at 311.14, showing consistent uptrend from 306 support. Financials have been resilient and JPM is the highest quality name in the sector.
LLY shows a strong +1.5% today and +1.48% over 30 days from 939 to 957, with the chart trending above its 20-SMA. Healthcare defensive positioning adds portfolio diversification.
AAPL is at its 30-day high at 260.54 with consistent upward trend from 256 support. The chart shows price above a rising SMA with +1.0% today, suggesting continued momentum into close.
GOOGL is up +1.6% today and leads the megacap tech names on the day, with +1.58% over 30 days and a clear trend from 311 to 319 support. Price is near 30-day highs with solid volume.

HD shows +2.8% today and is at its 30-day high of 340.33, with a strong uptrend from 328 support. The chart shows price consistently above the 20-SMA with rising momentum — a breakout to new highs is bullish.

AMZN is the strongest performer today at +3.4% with a clear uptrend over the 30-day chart — price has moved from ~224 to 233 above a rising SMA. The 3.4% 30D gain with high average volume confirms institutional accumulation.

HD shows strong momentum with +2.10% 30D% and +0.96% 5D%, indicating upward trend and potential for further gains; adding this position diversifies into consumer discretionary with positive performance.
COST has minimal gains (+0.31% unrealized) and low volatility; partial trim reduces weight while maintaining some exposure to consumer staples, aligning with a more balanced portfolio.
V has shown slight negative momentum with a -0.01% 1D% change and is underperforming relative to other financials; trimming this position frees up cash and reduces exposure to a weaker performer.
LLY is exhibiting strong relative strength with a clear chart uptrend and +1.66% return over the last 30 days. Allocating the capital freed from UNH to capture this momentum.
UNH has been range-bound and is significantly underperforming its sector peers over the last 30 days. Exiting to free up capital for stronger momentum plays.

JPM is also pressing its 30-day high after a steady climb, but the move is less explosive than other leaders and financials generally offer less upside torque than the strongest tech/consumer names today. A partial trim reduces concentration and creates flexibility for later intraday opportunities.

NVDA is near its 30-day high, but today's advance is modest and the intraday chart looks more sideways than the stronger breakout patterns seen in GOOGL, AMZN, or CAT. With the portfolio fully invested, trimming here harvests gains and frees capital without exiting a still-healthy trend.
UNH has been the weakest performer - essentially flat over 30 days (+0.06%) with only +0.28% unrealized gain. Range-bound chart with no clear catalyst. Trimming to redeploy into stronger names.
AMZN shows robust performance with +3.50% today and +6.23% over 5 days, supported by high average volume; scaling up position for continued upside.
GOOGL is up +3.88% today and +10.35% over 5 days with positive 30D% return; increasing weight to capitalize on strong momentum in communication services.
XOM has declined -4.69% today and -7.92% over 5 days, showing weakness in energy sector; trimming to free up capital for better opportunities.

The portfolio currently has 11 open positions, exceeding the maximum of 10, due to a tiny residual holding in UNH with negligible value and weight. Fully exiting UNH brings the portfolio into compliance without any material impact on overall value or strategy, as it contributes 0.00% to the portfolio.
UNH is exhibiting significant relative strength within the healthcare sector with a 13% gain over the last 5 days, powerfully reversing its previous downtrend.
GOOGL has shown a powerful 5-day rally of over 10%, breaking above its 20-period SMA and showing strong relative momentum compared to mega-cap peers.
JNJ continues to lag both the broader market and sector peers, trading sideways to down with negative 5-day and 30-day returns.
XOM has experienced a sharp 5-day decline of nearly 8%, breaking support and showing a negative shift in trend momentum relative to the broader market.

GOOGL has strong multi-horizon momentum, up 10.35% over 5 days and back near its 30-day high. The chart shows a sharp rebound off the recent low and a move back above the short-term trend, making it a good candidate to absorb capital from weaker sectors.

XOM has the weakest short-term profile in the portfolio, down 4.7% on the day and nearly 8% over 5 days, with the chart showing a rollover from recent highs. Relative strength has deteriorated versus both SPY and the rest of our holdings, so I prefer to fully exit the small residual position.
LLY shows strong momentum with +1.74% 30D return and positive 1D% change, reflecting growth in healthcare and high conviction from recent price action.
WMT exhibits robust performance with +2.48% 30D return and consistent gains, indicating defensive strength and stable upward trend in consumer staples.
XOM has strong momentum with +1.67% 30D return and high average volume, supported by positive energy sector trends and recent price strength.
CAT shows minimal gains (+0.09% 30D) and low average volume, suggesting limited upside potential; exiting allows reallocation to more dynamic stocks.
UNH has underperformed with a -1.32% 30D return and negative 1D% change, indicating weak momentum; trimming this position frees up capital for better opportunities.

The portfolio has 11 open positions exceeding the maximum of 10, primarily due to a tiny residual holding in UNH with negligible value; fully exiting this position corrects the violation without affecting overall portfolio allocation or performance.
WMT is a standout leader in consumer staples with a very steady 2.48% gain over 30 days, trading near its highs and demonstrating persistent buying pressure.
Energy is showing clear relative strength, and XOM is breaking out to the upside with a strong 1.49% 5-day gain, tracking nicely above its 20-period SMA.
JNJ is showing a pristine, low-volatility uptrend over the 30-period chart with strong momentum (+2.18% over 30 days), making it an excellent defensive addition.
META is experiencing a sharp pullback from its recent 30-day highs, with consecutive red candles indicating short-term distribution and risking further mean reversion.
GOOGL is exhibiting flat to negative momentum over the last 30 days and has been struggling to maintain sustained strength above its moving average. Capital is better deployed elsewhere.

XOM is showing strong multi-horizon momentum (+1.49% over 5 days, +1.67% over 30 days) and is trading near its 30-day high, with the chart confirming an upward trend above the moving average. A modest 3% allocation uses the freed capital to add exposure to one of the clearest relative-strength setups in the universe.

UNH has the weakest 30-day performance in the current portfolio at -1.32% and remains close to its 30-day low, with the chart showing persistent relative weakness versus the market. Exiting the small 3% position frees capital from a laggard without materially increasing turnover.
LLY has gained +1.74% over 30 days, with its chart showing a strong uptrend above the 20-period SMA and recent highs. This positions it well for potential continued growth in the healthcare sector.

UNH has declined -1.32% over the past 30 days with a downward chart trend breaking below its 20-period SMA, suggesting continued weakness. Exiting the position frees up capital and a slot for better opportunities.
XOM is breaking out of a recent consolidation phase with strong 5-day momentum. I am initiating a starter position to capture this upward move.
GOOGL has been range-bound and is struggling to establish a clear trend above its 20-period SMA. I am trimming the position to free up capital.
WMT displays an exceptionally strong and consistent uptrend, trading right at its 30-day high. This is a high-conviction momentum play.
UNH is stuck in a persistent 30-day downtrend and is trading near its recent lows. I am exiting the position to stop further drag on the portfolio.

META remains one of the stronger large-cap growth names on a 30-day basis and the chart shows a sharp rebound followed by constructive consolidation above the rising SMA. Adding modestly keeps exposure in a demonstrated leader while staying below concentration limits.

WMT is at or essentially touching a fresh 30-day high with strong 30-day relative performance and a clean upward trend above its moving average. The chart shows steady accumulation rather than a one-bar spike, which supports increasing the position.

GOOGL has flat-to-negative performance across 1D, 5D, and 30D windows and is trading below its 30-day high with muted momentum. Trimming frees capital from a lagging mega-cap into names showing stronger trend persistence.

universe.description: Default 20-name universe: mega-cap leaders across all major sectors. Liquid, well-covered, large-cap U.S. equities.
